slide-01.text: Food safety without blind spots. I'm here to propose a digital platform for eco-wires. One that delivers always on assurance across every site, every region, every shift, and every day. Why is that even important? Well, for global food brands, hygiene and sanitation is a broad level risk, not a line item. It is something that cannot be delegated. Accountability always sits right at the top.

segments.text: Food safety without blind spots.
I'm here to propose a digital platform for eco-wires.
One that delivers always on assurance across every site, every region, every shift, and
every day.
Why is that even important?
Well, for global food brands, hygiene and sanitation is a broad level risk, not a line
item.
It is something that cannot be delegated.
Accountability always sits right at the top.

slide-02.text: Historically, there is an accountability gap. And what is that accountability gap? So the people who execute the hygiene and sanitation protocols on site are ultimately not the people held accountable. See, we delegate this responsibility to them, but ultimately cannot delegate accountability for anything going wrong. Without continuous visibility, leadership relies on periodic reports and site visits to assess and mitigate any such risks. Site managers and supervisors do the work, compiles all the reports, but otherwise top management holds the ultimate accountability. Today, that accountability relies on our trust on the people on site, as it should be, but in our industry, that trust requires that extra burden of proof. And top management can only manage what they know.

slide-03.text: If it isn't recorded, then it didn't happen. That's a golden rule. So how confident are we that when incidents happen on site, management has done the due diligence. The recorded records as and when required. When there is that mock recall, that unannounced audit outside or physically visiting the site, how do we know? How can we be proactive about it? Executives need evidence that is time-stemmed, traceable, and defendable, even in terms of litigation. A digital platform transformed paper doubt into digital proof.

slide-04.text: One incident has potential for enterprise impact. A single hygiene and sanitation failure can cascade into a product recall, lost business, regulatory action, and reputational damage. The exposure multiplies with every site and every customer. A single incident at any site reflects on your brand. This is about brand protection.

segments.text: One incident has potential for enterprise impact.
A single hygiene and sanitation failure can cascade into a product recall, lost business,
regulatory action, and reputational damage.
The exposure multiplies with every site and every customer.
A single incident at any site reflects on your brand.
This is about brand protection.

slide-05.text: There is an inherent problem with manual records that audit drag and a decision lag. It obscures trends until they become incidents. Documents are piled in binders and in file cabinets. By the time leadership sees any data, the risk is already manifested. Leaders need faster decisions and fewer surprises.

segments.text: There is an inherent problem with manual records that audit drag and a decision lag.
It obscures trends until they become incidents.
Documents are piled in binders and in file cabinets.
By the time leadership sees any data, the risk is already manifested.
Leaders need faster decisions and fewer surprises.

slide-06.text: The digital platform proposed would have four pillars, verify cleaning at point of work, correct issues with automatic escalation, audit with standardized templates and scoring, and oversee everything from a multi-site command center. A unified system that tends sanitation into a live, auditable operation instead of a monthly snapshot. MobileFest, offline capable and secure.

segments.text: The digital platform proposed would have four pillars, verify cleaning at point of work,
correct issues with automatic escalation, audit with standardized templates and scoring,
and oversee everything from a multi-site command center.
A unified system that tends sanitation into a live, auditable operation instead of a
monthly snapshot. MobileFest, offline capable and secure.

slide-07.text: Now let's talk about the cleaning verification module, which is a module that is at the core of this digital platform. What it does in essence is to transform the master cleaning schedule that you agree with your clients in the SLAs. It takes an entire area looking at what needs to be cleaned in that area, and then transforming that into a digital task. Now what you put in that digital task is entirely up to you, but that's then what is available to the guys on site. It typically addresses the what, the when, the how, the who, specs, corrective actions, even if you want to take it to a level where you attach standards as photographs to say, this is what a clean mixer looks like. These digital tasks are then attached to some form of proof of presence at the site, where you can use things like QR accords, bar accords, NFC tags, RFID tags. And this would demand that the guys on site activate a proof of presence that shows indeed I was in this area and was able to sign off these digital tasks that speak to our SLA.

slide-08.text: The digital platform would not be complete without an NCR management system. So what we do with this NCR management system is to capture your framework of how you deal with non-confirmities, how you rate the severity, how you want them resolved, the timelines, your framework for communication, who is responsible, accountable, consulted, or informed, or whatever you want to do that. But in essence, we are able to give a full audit trail and status levels of every NCR. Where is it sitting? What has been resolved? What is outstanding? What has been put in a parking bay?

slide-09.text: Looking at internal audits, which is part of this digital platform, what we do with this is to take your internal audits, turn them into a digital audit that your area managers and operations guys can complete whilst they are on site. This internal audit module is linked again to the NCR module, fully linked for full traceability, and as digital signatures, offline capability, historical comparisons and trends, which can show improvement over time. And I think an excellent point of engagement with your clients, base your engagements on fact, on the scores of your internal audits, which are immediately available on completion to your clients to have a view of.

slide-10.text: The Executive Command Center is that single dashboard that gives you visibility of all your key activities on site. So this will include your compliance scores per site. It could well be grouped per client group, grouped per region, whatever you want to do, but with the ability to still drill down right to the individual areas. It gives you your NCR accounts, your trends, your aging severity across the business, trend analysis showing whether sites are improving or in a declining state allows you to take faster decisions and fewer surprises. This is the visibility and accountability that is required.

slide-11.text: Here is our understanding of your business and your needs. Equalize is trusted by food safety leaders. And you've built this trust over 25 years and in different countries. We understand what is at stake. We understand the nature of the food safety industry and the global brands that you service. As a market leader and a thought leader in this space, we are excited to engage with you in building your vision for a digital platform. We are excited to know what it is that you have us build.

slide-12.text: So, what is it that we bring to the table? We have capacity to build and scale, cleaning verifications, NEPRP programs like Glass Registers, NCR Management, internal audits, customer complaints, satisfaction surveys, proof of presence, asset registers, online training, document control and other related modules.

segments.text: So, what is it that we bring to the table?
We have capacity to build and scale, cleaning verifications, NEPRP programs like Glass Registers,
NCR Management, internal audits, customer complaints, satisfaction surveys, proof of
presence, asset registers, online training, document control and other related modules.

slide-13.text: The proposal is we co-build and you own this. We work with your teams to make work flows and build what fits your operations. We will complement your deep food safety expertise and provide scalable architecture and a responsive team that understands what is at stake, what it means for records to be available every time. And crucially, what we build together is EcoWise's IP. The platform and intellectual property remain yours, protecting your competitive advantage as we go forward.

slide-14.text: What is this transformation? Well, paper is reactive and digital is proactive. With paper records, you've got your files logged on site, inside some filing cabinets. Digital makes all these records accessible from any device anyway for all stakeholders. Paper cannot prove what happened at 2 AM when you have that mock recall, where you've got that actual recall. But with digital, you provide time-stemmed, geolocated, photo-verified evidence. Paper, NCRs get lost or delayed in paper systems. Digital delivers automatic escalation. Paper needs your operations management to visit sites for visibility. What digital provides a combined center that is always on and that is available, trends available for all stakeholders.

slide-15.text: In conclusion, let's build this together. A platform where you still delegate responsibility at site level. You can't run away from that. But now you retain accountability at the top whilst seeing everything. Always own assurance. Food safety without blind spots. Thank you.
